mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate wage income, which drives consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS growth months is not significantly above 50 percent (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes consumption contraction and earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 45 percent of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of an elevated federal funds rate with high CPIAUCSL inflation raises real borrowing costs and erodes household purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns through a discount-rate and earnings double hit.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction term remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the bivariate model's directional accuracy does not exceed the best univariate benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in industrial production predicts negative S&P 500 returns during manufacturing-led slowdowns because falling capacity utilization and pricing power erode corporate margins and trigger earnings revisions.
failure_condition: During manufacturing-led slowdowns, month-over-month INDPRO declines coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling more than 10 percent below their trailing 12-month average signal a deep contraction in residential construction and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals accommodative liquidity conditions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or FEDFUNDS decline months do not yield a higher proportion of positive 5-day returns than a random baseline (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirms a self-reinforcing demand-and-income feedback loop that lifts corporate earnings visibility, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term's Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform a univariate RSAFS model in directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Back-to-back monthly declines in industrial production signal an unfolding industrial recession that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
